review_title: Effective collaboration made easy
description: Very positive. Has helped us to collaborate and share ideas and brain storm far more effectively. Works great for our hybrid teams.
author: 🇺🇦 Robert R.
pros: Its quick and easy to build ideas, diagrams and helpful to communicate especially for remote and hybrid teams. The quick ability to go into the "Lucid" mode to draw more complex diagrams and images is great. The team exercises is a brilliant features that helps collaboration massively.
cons: Permissions and sharing is complicated and it isn't always clear which is the best way to share. Not everyone in our organization has lucid and its sometimes tricky to collaborate. Its possible but you have to use a specific share mode (with password) and so that can cause friction.

review_title: LucidSpark - Amazing Program
description: It is a great product. I'm still figuring out how to utilize it more for my business. Inspiration isn't always instantaneous. I know my employees have liked it but probably not as much as I have.
author: Paul B.
pros: I think my favorite thing is the mind mapping capability. It also allows me to share my maps with other people in the office. Although the other people did not collaborate as I had hoped, they were able to see my thoughts on different topics for out business meeting.
cons: In my other mind mapping program (X-Mind), I am able to collapse points down and expand them out easily. This can be done programmatically right now but I didn't want to create layers and program this.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great diagram drawing tool
description: I like lucidspark because it is more flexible when drawing diagrams compare to lucidchart, also drawing are more smooth too.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Lucidspark is a total game-changer for project collaboration, making it feel like my team is in the same room even when we're miles apart. The features are surprisingly intuitive, especially the way it lets us turn a messy brainstorm into a structured plan with just a few clicks. It’s a massive productivity booster because we no longer waste time trying to translate "whiteboard photos" into actual tasks. While it’s not the cheapest option out there, the value for money is clear since it replaces three or four other clunky tools. Honestly, it’s the best investment we’ve made for keeping our creative momentum alive.
cons: If lucidspark can provide a better AI way to generate diagram, that will be great, or have a template for use to use.

review_title: The Paywall Is Ridiculous
author: Danielle D.
pros: Lucidspark is a great app. I use the app for personal use and the documents are very secure, my data hasn't been leaked. I personally haven't had to use the customer support, but I'm sure its great. It's effortless to manage my projects. The features are very diverse and work as intended. The collaboration is by far it's best feature, works extremely well and has little to no bugs.
cons: The subscription price is ridiculous, $108 per year. The worst part about the subscription service is that they limit you to three separate whiteboards on the free plan. That is redundant, how are you supposed to use this for work-related purposes on a free trial, for those who can't afford such a fee?

review_title: Lucidspark points of interest and improvements
description: It has been okay for the price point it’s been set at though I do wish there was more meaningful integration of multiple screens windows or inputs such as a computer and laptop
author: Naya O.
pros: I like how fluid the use of the whiteboard is for visualization of math concepts and helps improve some learning techniques of students who rely on the visual cues
cons: Importing files to access on the whiteboard can be difficult and leads to weird sizing orientation or quality of the file
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Lucid - a great digital whiteboard option
description: Overall, lucid is great to work with. We transitioned from Miro to Lucid and have found the transition easy and love the features that lucid offers
author: Heather K.
pros: It's a great way to collaborate as a team - lots of great features that are very intuitive to use. I especially like the activities feature that makes lucid interactive with users - allowing us to run surveys and get ratings in real time
cons: The lack of community - having used miro in the past I really miss the bank of boards and tools you could use in the miroverse. Lucid has some templates, but not the same range that miro offers
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy to use + collaborate!
description: Using Lucidspark is a dynamic and engaging experience, especially for teams focused on collaboration and creativity. The platform offers a flexible digital canvas where users can brainstorm ideas, map out processes, and work together in real time.
author: Zsofia S.
pros: Lucidspark is an intuitive, cloud-based virtual whiteboard that enhances team collaboration and brainstorming. It allows users to sketch, organize, and refine ideas in real time, making it ideal for remote or hybrid teams. With features like sticky notes, voting, comments, and templates, it supports agile workflows and creative ideation.
cons: Despite its strengths, Lucidspark may be overwhelming for new users unfamiliar with digital whiteboarding tools. Its rich feature set can lead to cluttered boards if not managed well, reducing clarity. Performance can lag with large or complex boards, particularly on lower-end devices.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great mouse-usable tool
author: Ankur R.
pros: I think while it wasn't the most intuitive to use at first, the ability to express my ideas visually, and the fact that it is so mouse-optimized is what I like best about it.
cons: I'd say there's a whole lot of the UI that I don't use just because I don't have a use case for them, I would like to hide them in some way.

review_title: Lucidspark is essential for remote working!
description: Really love lucidspark, it is an essential tool for the remote working world. Allows you to continue to whiteboard in virtual meetings where everyone can see it, and it never has to be erased when the meeting is over!
author: Aaron H.
pros: Lucidspark is elite at making meetings collaborative and visual for everyone! Has great different features and visuals to keep people engaged and organized.
cons: Lucidspark has a slight learning curve to it and can be a little awkward the first couple meetings when it is being used. Once you get over the hump, you engagement will be so much better than before though!

review_title: Highly recommend!!!
description: Absolutely fantastic experience! Integrates with all my company software seamlessly. Love LucidSpark!
author: Holly H.
pros: User friendly. Easy to integrate with draw.io code file. I thought I would have a difficult time as my diagram was ion a draw.io file, but I just upload the file and LucidSpark did the rest! Highly recommend.
cons: There is absolutely nothing I would change or recommend to make this a better user experience. LucidSpark is the best.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: LucidSpark Review
author: Jason Y.
pros: I enjoyed Lucidspark’s interactive brainstorming features, which made visual collaboration feel really natural. Its clean interface and sticky-note style elements helped capture ideas quickly without feeling cluttered
cons: Sometimes the board can become unwieldy if too many team members are adding content at once. Additionally, the cost might be a concern for smaller teams looking for more robust features without a high price tag.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Lucid spark review
description: Really positive and has enabled me to work virtually more efficiently
author: Claudia H.
pros: The ease of interactivity and how it has everything you need in one whiteboard and the templates that can be used and amended to your needs
cons: There's no simple cheat sheet for facilitators of the whiteboard to share with workshop attendees to have a quick lesson on how to use

review_title: Lucidspark is a powerful flexible mapping tool but not the industry leader
description: Lucidspark was valuable to us at a time but ultimately was replaced by Miro.
author: Lucien H.
pros: The most powerful value proposition Lucidspark has is it is owned and built by Lucidchart. Being able to migrate a whole library of charts when Lucidspark came out was incredibly valuable for us when mapping become a lot more dynamic. Lucidchart collaboration is also excellent sharing lucidsparks and communicating over comments.
cons: Lucidspark is a very expensive tool, when you take into consideration already paying for Lucidcharts. We liked the tools but it didn't match up to the agile fluid design and implementation of Miro. Miro quickly replaced Lucidspark as a more affordable solution that offered the same, if not more power, alongside a community driven map library.

review_title: An great whiteboard that helps us collaborate to find the best ideas.
description: Lucidspark enabled us to cooperate more effectively, and kept our meetings always open so that we could express our opinions on all proposals at any time, which helped us a lot by collecting all ideas and linking them properly.
author: Ahmed F.
pros: Lucidspark is the best whiteboard linked to the cloud, where we can easily access it and also the user interface is easy to understand, where we can find all the tools we need effortlessly. I really appreciate the huge capabilities that Lucidspark provides us with, which makes collaborating with the rest of the team on preparing workflow diagrams as well as collecting ideas very enjoyable. I like very much using shapes, adding texts and images, coordinating them, and also adding certain colors to them so that they indicate either suggested modifications or additions and notes that you have included on the original form. I very much appreciate the sharing capabilities of the proposed models, as there are many options, which eliminates the need for us to use auxiliary programs for that. Also, the ready-made models help us a lot and give us a lot of ideas that help us quickly implement the schemes that we need.
cons: I didn't find anything to dislike about Lucidspark, as it's intuitive and easy to use for everyone with different occupations, and I find it very responsive compared to other similar apps.

review_title: Excellent strategic planning tool in the form of a virtual whiteboard.
description: Lucidspark is primarily used by me for long-term planning. The most useful aspect was that I was able to immediately use icons to explain my planning.
author: Waleed K.
pros: Working in a group using Lucidspark is a breez, where We no longer need to meet in person to take images of the whiteboard. I used the workflow databases and diagrams to develop a whole application, which Start using one of the many templates provided. There's lots of opportunity for thought and debate here, for that we Make it unique with the available shapes.
cons: Disappointing was the large size of the completed workflow drawings, which we were sometimes unable to email as attachments.

review_title: What is it about Lucidspark?
description: Our team and partners from all over the world can collaborate and brainstorm anywhere they are on the globe.
author: Joel B.
pros: Most impactful features would be the remote collaboration benefit of Lucidspark and the integration with tools like Slack and Microsoft Teams.
cons: There is a steep learning curve if you haven't used any of such tools before. So usually we give our staff a little tutorial before they use it.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Wonderful tool for professionals
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Till now whatever I have seen and presented using the Lucid, it always a great experience, in less time and effort, it delivers quality.
cons: As of now nothing is bad if you have a paid account else one will Not able to edit the shared files.

review_title: Low-key replacement for post-it-notes and whiteboards
description: Lucidspark, like its sibling, Lucidchart, is intuitive and easy to use. You can get up to speed with using this software very quickly and with next to no training.
author: George K.
pros: This software is amazing for aggregating information and ideas and cataloging them in a meaningful way. I love how you can connect similar ideas and information to see connections and relationships. This has been very useful for capturing the wisdom and experience of retiring employees so it can be conveyed to personnel coming up through the ranks. Being able to catalog their different responsibilities and connect them together allows us to present a holistic view of the program so that new employees can get up to speed faster. I also love how this software is browser based so that it can be used anywhere on any device. This frees up staff from having to be in the office or worrying if they have a company computer.
cons: I would like to see a feature where you can attach documents to different sparks. It would be helpful to enrich different ideas and sticky notes with supporting documents.

review_title: Flexible Easy to use Collaborative Tool
author: Alexander F.
pros: For the first time in my life, I was able to collaborate with multiple stakeholders, all thanks to Lucid Spark. The drawing tools are impressively advanced, and the workspace is vey well organized
cons: As far as I have been using it, I don't recall ever having any problems with Lucidspark.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Good software to express your ideas with schematics
description: I started to use it for wireframes, but then I started to use it for flow charts and other schemes
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Really intuitive and fairly full of features. It helps me from mind maps to website wireframes
cons: Not really, it does what it has to do in an easy way

review_title: Great tool, especially for remote work and collaboration
description: The overall experience has been great.
author: Alexandra G.
pros: I like that is extremely easy to dive in and start using right away. It is very simple to find quick wins and 100s of ways to use the product. It's been a great tool for our team
cons: I don't have anything that is a deterrent for this product. It works well and is easy to use. I do notice that when I use it for client calls, we have others that seem to "look" better but that's not a make it or break it.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: See your ideas come to life
description: Exciting, inspiring, and such fun to use.
author: Bridget R.
pros: So easy to get started without any training. Very intuitive and the templates make it possible to produce something very professional looking and useful in a short space of time. Loved seeing my ideas come to life in colour and being able to share them with my coach immediately for feedback. Fab piece of software - love it!
cons: Too early to say. Didn't like the arrows between the bubbles at first but then got the hang of that.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Simply the best flowcharting and wireframing tool
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's very easy to build flowcharts and process flows with this browser-based tool. I use it mostly for creating marketing automation workflows.
cons: The color coding process could be a bit clunky. Once you get the hang of it you're fine.

review_title: Overall, a good experience with the tools and functionalities of Lucid Spark
description: A good experience in the past few months as we use to track the project status.
author: Lekhya D.
pros: The ease of Development and the main reason that we use this is as part of our development is to track the project status.
cons: We can improve the functions which we use to create a workflow and all, and can also include more icons to make a better UI
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Business Analyist
author: Randall J.
pros: Ease of use and powerful collaboration tools
cons: Some times adjusting flows over swim lanes can be a little tricky
